title: Badge Component
content:
The `Badge` component is a reusable UI element used to display status, labels, or categories. ## Key Exports * **`Badge`**: The main React component for rendering badges. * **`badgeVariants`**: A `class-variance-authority` (cva) function that generates CSS classes based on defined variants. ## Props ### `BadgeProps` Extends standard `React.HTMLAttributes` and includes `VariantProps` from `badgeVariants`. * `className` (string): Additional CSS classes to apply to the badge. * `variant` (enum): Controls the visual style of the badge. Possible values are: * `default` (default): Primary colored badge. * `secondary`: Secondary colored badge. * `destructive`: Destructive colored badge (e.g., for errors or warnings). * `outline`: Badge with an outline and no background fill. * `...props` (React.HTMLAttributes): Any other standard HTML div attributes. ## Usage Example ```jsx import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge'; function MyComponent() { return (
Default Badge Secondary Badge Destructive Badge Outline Badge
); } ``` ## `badgeVariants` This function is used internally by the `Badge` component to apply styling based on the `variant` prop. It's also exportable if you need to apply badge styles directly to other elements or for custom styling scenarios.
